inclusion_criteria: Original, peer-reviewed human study using an observational design (e.g., cross-sectional case–control or cohort) or baseline data from an intervention study that compares medication-free MDD patients to healthy controls

Population includes patients with a clinical diagnosis of unipolar major depressive disorder who are medication-free at the time of MRI (medication-naïve or off all psychotropic medications with an adequate washout); controls are healthy without psychiatric diagnoses
Cortical thickness is measured with structural MRI (e.g., surface-based morphometry) and thickness outcomes are reported for at least one cortical region or whole-brain
exclusion_criteria: Participants using psychotropic medication at the time of scanning or medication status not clearly medication-free
No healthy control group, or mixed/other primary diagnoses (e.g., bipolar disorder, psychotic disorders) or major neurological comorbidities without separable MDD-only data
Imaging modality/outcome not appropriate (e.g., no MRI-based cortical thickness; only volume or surface area; DTI, fMRI, PET) or non-human studies
Non-original or insufficient reports (e.g., reviews, meta-analyses, editorials, letters, conference abstracts, preprints) or duplicate datasets
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to include human studies comparing medication-free unipolar MDD patients to healthy controls with cortical thickness measured by structural MRI.

included_articles.title: Increased prefrontal and parietal cortical thickness does not correlate with anhedonia in patients with untreated first-episode major depressive disorders.
Sex-specific alterations of cortical morphometry in treatment-naïve patients with major depressive disorder.
Regional increases of cortical thickness in untreated, first-episode major depressive disorder.
Surface-based analysis of early cortical gyrification and thickness alterations in treatment-Naïve, first-episode depressive patients during emerging adulthood.
